ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for crabstick (surimi) salad; 'seafood salad food product' is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for surimi/crabstick salad; A0CJW (crustaceans and products thereof, processed) is the closest defensible parent. Crabsticks are surimi-based imitation crab, not true crustacean, but this is the nearest available category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept identified for crabstick salad or surimi salad as a prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of crabstick salad consists of 6 pieces, weighing approximately 120 g, typically served chilled as a starter or side dish at a Japanese sushi restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Crab, imitation, made from surimi (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB 15136); Salad dressing, mayonnaise-type (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB 04025); composite estimation for crabstick salad dish
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a prepared crabstick salad dish. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for imitation crab (surimi) at approximately 70% of dish weight and mayonnaise dressing at approximately 20%, with cucumber and salad leaves at approximately 10%, then normalising to per-100 g. Energy cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central Foundation Foods entries for surimi and mayonnaise components.

gi_source.title: Surimi (imitation crab) GI analogous estimate based on: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for crabstick salad. GI estimated at 40 based on the GI of surimi/imitation crab products (low GI, predominantly protein and starch binder) combined with the low-GI contribution of mayonnaise dressing (fat-dominant, GI ~0) and salad vegetables (GI ~15). The composite dish GI is estimated at 40 (low category). Glycemic load per 120 g serving = (40 × 10.5 × 1.20) / 100 ≈ 5.0 g. Estimation basis documented per Veritas v2 GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from surimi processing and mayonnaise
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size of crabstick salad, as a 120 g serving provides approximately 576 mg sodium (roughly 25% of the 2,300 mg daily limit), primarily from the sodium-rich surimi base and mayonnaise dressing.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and moderate carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Crabstick salad has a low estimated glycemic index (~40) and a glycemic load of approximately 5 per serving, making it a suitable occasional choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without additional high-GI accompaniments such as white rice.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Sodium load and mayonnaise-derived fat content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The elevated sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g) and the saturated fat contribution from mayonnaise warrant moderation for individuals with cardiovascular disease; choosing a reduced-fat mayonnaise variant or smaller portions can help manage cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Crabstick Salad (6 pieces) is a chilled Japanese-style appetiser or side dish commonly served at sushi restaurants and Japanese casual dining establishments in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. The dish consists of six pieces of crabstick — made from surimi, a processed fish paste (typically pollock) shaped and flavoured to resemble crab meat — dressed with Japanese-style mayonnaise and served alongside shredded cucumber, lettuce, or mixed salad leaves. Sesame seeds are sometimes added as a garnish. The texture of crabstick is soft, slightly chewy, and mildly sweet, with a delicate imitation-crab flavour. The mayonnaise dressing adds a creamy, rich coating. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate protein (~6.8 g per 100 g) from the surimi base, moderate fat (~9.2 g per 100 g) predominantly from mayonnaise, and a relatively low carbohydrate content (~10.5 g per 100 g) from the starch binders in surimi. Sodium content is elevated (~480 mg per 100 g) due to the processing of surimi and the salt content of mayonnaise, which is a clinical consideration for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The estimated glycemic index is low (~40), making it a reasonable choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when portion-controlled. Allergens include fish (surimi base), egg (mayonnaise), and wheat (surimi starch binder). This dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with fish or egg allergies.
common_user_queries: How many calories in crabstick salad 6 pieces?
Is crabstick salad suitable for diabetics?
Is crabstick salad high in sodium?
Can I eat crabstick salad if I have high blood pressure?
What allergens are in crabstick salad?
How much protein is in crabstick salad?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for a prepared crabstick salad dish as served in a restaurant context. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for surimi (imitation crab) and mayonnaise components, normalised to per-100 g. GI is an analogous estimate based on peer-reviewed GI tables for surimi-based products. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for surimi (NDB 15136) and mayonnaise-type dressing (NDB 04025), with salad vegetables contributing minor amounts. No direct approved-source entry for a prepared crabstick salad dish was identified. 2. Serving size of 120 g is estimated based on a typical 6-piece restaurant portion at Sushi Express Singapore; actual weight may vary by restaurant. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/trace in surimi and standard mayonnaise per USDA data. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries for surimi or mayonnaise used as the basis for this estimation. 5. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for crabstick salad was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 6. Halal status is listed as false because standard surimi may contain non-halal additives and the restaurant (Sushi Express) is not certified halal; this should be verified at the restaurant level. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component data and should be treated as indicative rather than precise. 8. food_group corrected from input value 'Vegetables' to 'Fish and seafood' as the primary ingredient is surimi (processed fish product).

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected food_group from 'Vegetables' (input) to 'Fish and seafood' (correct classification for surimi-based dish).

Estimated nutrient values from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data (surimi NDB 15136, mayonnaise NDB 04025) as no direct approved-source entry exists for this prepared dish.
Set vitamin_d_mcg to -1 with explicit documentation in notes as required by G3 sentinel value rule.
Set trans_fat_g to 0.0 with justification (confirmed negligible in USDA data for components).
Estimated GI at 40 (low) using analogous estimate from Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed GI tables; fully documented in gi_source.
